(P2) Determinism: for two DISTINCT marker strings to tie on source_id length they would have to start at the same offset in one string, which is impossible; the legacy endswith branch likewise admits at most one suffix (no legacy suffix is a suffix of another) -> the PYTHONHASHSEED nondeterminism is removed in practice. (P3) False-admit reachability: a valid candidate equal to '<expected>'/'brick-protocol-<expected>' requires report_id == '<expected>-<suffix>-<valid-ts>' i.e. literally expected's own legit report; no foreign building emits it -> no false-admit path found. (P4) False-reject: legit forms parse to brick-protocol-<bid> because nothing marker-like follows the genuine trailing marker.
